mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when either series is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations during early-cycle expansions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC > 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth and higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following GDP growth, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction sector activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.
